content_en:
Hilyat al-Awliya' wa Tabaqat al-Asfiya' — "Adornment of the Friends of God and Classes of the Pure" — is organized chronologically by generation (tabaqah), beginning with the Companions of the Prophet and proceeding through the Successors (Tabi'un), the Successors of the Successors, and subsequent generations down to Abu Nu'aym's own era. This generational structure emphasizes the continuity of the tradition of piety from the prophetic era through the centuries.

For each individual, Abu Nu'aym provides biographical information and then, most distinctively, quotations of their sayings, accounts of their devotional practices, descriptions of their character, and stories illustrating their spiritual states. These accounts are transmitted with isnads (chains of narration), applying the same documentary standards to spiritual biography that hadith scholars applied to prophetic traditions. This methodological choice was central to Abu Nu'aym's project: by presenting the accounts of the awliya' (friends of God) with proper hadith-style documentation, he integrated them into the mainstream of Islamic transmitted knowledge.

The range of individuals included is broad. Abu Nu'aym includes among the awliya' not only figures who would later be classified as Sufi masters — Hasan al-Basri, Rabi'ah al-Adawiyyah, Ibrahim ibn Adham, Bishr al-Hafi, al-Junayd, and others — but also major hadith scholars, ascetics of various types, early jurists, and other figures distinguished for their piety rather than mystical teaching per se. This breadth reflects his conception of the "friends of God" as encompassing all those who lived their religion with exemplary depth.

The saying collections — apophthegmata, wisdom sayings attributed to each figure — are among the most valuable parts of the work. Many of these sayings are not preserved elsewhere in such complete form, and the Hilyat al-Awliya' is the primary source for important statements attributed to early Sufi and ascetic figures. These collections influenced subsequent Sufi literature enormously, being quoted in later texts like al-Qushayri's Risalah and al-Ghazali's Ihya' Ulum ad-Din.

The scale of the work is remarkable — the standard modern edition fills ten substantial volumes. This size reflects both the breadth of Abu Nu'aym's inclusion criteria and his thoroughness in documenting each individual with multiple accounts.
content_id:
Hilyat al-Awliya wa Thabaqat al-Asfiya — Perhiasan Para Wali Allah dan Tingkatan-tingkatan Orang-orang Suci — disusun secara kronologis berdasarkan generasi (thabaqah), dimulai dari para Sahabat Nabi dan berlanjut melalui para Tabi'un, para Tabi'ut Tabi'in, dan generasi-generasi berikutnya hingga era Abu Nu'aim sendiri. Struktur generasional ini menekankan kesinambungan tradisi kesalehan dari era kenabian melalui berabad-abad.

Untuk setiap individu, Abu Nu'aim menyediakan informasi biografi dan kemudian, yang paling khas, kutipan-kutipan ucapan mereka, catatan-catatan praktik ibadah mereka, deskripsi-deskripsi karakter mereka, dan kisah-kisah yang mengilustrasikan keadaan-keadaan spiritual mereka. Catatan-catatan ini diterima dengan sanad (rantai periwayatan), menerapkan standar-standar dokumenter yang sama pada biografi spiritual yang diterapkan ulama hadis pada tradisi-tradisi kenabian. Pilihan metodologis ini merupakan inti dari proyek Abu Nu'aim: dengan menyajikan catatan-catatan tentang para awliya (wali Allah) dengan dokumentasi bergaya hadis yang tepat, ia mengintegrasikan mereka ke dalam arus utama pengetahuan Islam yang diterima.

Rentang individu yang dimasukkan sangat luas. Abu Nu'aim memasukkan di antara para awliya bukan hanya tokoh-tokoh yang kemudian akan diklasifikasikan sebagai guru-guru sufi — Hasan al-Basri, Rabi'ah al-Adawiyyah, Ibrahim ibn Adham, Bisyr al-Hafi, al-Junayd, dan lainnya — tetapi juga ulama-ulama hadis utama, para zahid dari berbagai jenis, ahli-ahli fikih awal, dan tokoh-tokoh lain yang dibedakan karena kesalehan mereka daripada pengajaran mistis semata. Keluasan ini mencerminkan konsepsinya tentang wali Allah sebagai mencakup semua mereka yang menjalani agama mereka dengan kedalaman yang teladan.

Koleksi-koleksi ucapan — kata-kata hikmah yang dinisbatkan kepada setiap tokoh — adalah salah satu bagian paling berharga dari karya ini. Banyak ucapan-ucapan ini tidak dilestarikan di tempat lain dalam bentuk yang selengkap ini, dan Hilyat al-Awliya adalah sumber utama untuk pernyataan-pernyataan penting yang dinisbatkan kepada tokoh-tokoh sufi dan zahid awal. Koleksi-koleksi ini sangat mempengaruhi literatur sufi berikutnya, dikutip dalam teks-teks kemudian seperti Risalah al-Qusyairi dan Ihya Ulum ad-Din al-Ghazali.

Skala karya ini luar biasa — edisi modern standar memenuhi sepuluh jilid yang tebal. Ukuran ini mencerminkan baik luasnya kriteria inklusi Abu Nu'aim maupun ketuntasannya dalam mendokumentasikan setiap individu dengan berbagai riwayat.
content_ar:
Hilyat al-Awliya' wa Tabaqat al-Asfiya' — "Adornment of the Friends of God and Classes of the Pure" — is organized chronologically by generation (tabaqah), beginning with the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ and proceeding through the Successors (Tabi'un), the Successors of the Successors, and subsequent generations down to Abu Nu'aym's own era. This generational structure emphasizes the continuity of the tradition of piety from the prophetic era through the centuries.

For each individual, Abu Nu'aym provides biographical information and then, most distinctively, quotations of their sayings, accounts of their devotional practices, descriptions of their character, and stories illustrating their spiritual states. These accounts are transmitted with isnads (chains of narration), applying the same documentary standards to spiritual biography that الحديث scholars applied to prophetic traditions. This methodological choice was central to Abu Nu'aym's project: by presenting the accounts of the awliya' (friends of God) with proper الحديث-style documentation, he integrated them into the mainstream of Islamic transmitted knowledge.

The range of individuals included is broad. Abu Nu'aym includes among the awliya' not only figures who would later be classified as Sufi masters — Hasan al-Basri, Rabi'ah al-Adawiyyah, Ibrahim ibn Adham, Bishr al-Hafi, al-Junayd, and others — but also major الحديث scholars, ascetics of various types, early jurists, and other figures distinguished for their piety rather than mystical teaching per se. This breadth reflects his conception of the "friends of God" as encompassing all those who lived their religion with exemplary depth.

The saying collections — apophthegmata, wisdom sayings attributed to each figure — are among the most valuable parts of the work. Many of these sayings are not preserved elsewhere in such complete form, and the Hilyat al-Awliya' is the primary source for important statements attributed to early Sufi and ascetic figures. These collections influenced subsequent Sufi literature enormously, being quoted in later texts like al-Qushayri's Risalah and al-Ghazali's Ihya' Ulum ad-Din.

The scale of the work is remarkable — the standard modern edition fills ten substantial volumes. This size reflects both the breadth of Abu Nu'aym's inclusion criteria and his thoroughness in documenting each individual with multiple accounts.
